applied_optimizations.name: torch.compile with mode=default
applied_optimizations.description: Used torch.compile(mode='default') to let PyTorch inductor select the best matmul backend. The inductor autotune correctly chose rocBLAS (mm) at 0.9027ms over Triton kernels (best was 1.55ms).

attempted_but_failed.name: Manual Triton matmul kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: Triton kernel (1.45-1.70ms) cannot beat highly optimized rocBLAS/hipBLASLt (0.92ms) for this matrix size

attempted_but_failed.name: Triton autotune
attempted_but_failed.reason: Autotune selected configurations that produced incorrect results due to configuration mismatches

top_bottlenecks: GEMM is the only operation - already optimized by rocBLAS

notes: For simple GEMM operations on AMD MI355X, rocBLAS/hipBLASLt via torch.matmul is already optimal
Triton custom kernels cannot beat vendor-optimized libraries for standard matrix multiplication
torch.compile(mode='default') correctly dispatches to the best backend
Speedup achieved: ~1.0x (0.917ms vs 0.919ms baseline) - within measurement variance
